judgment.summary: UPS presents a mixed fundamental picture with attractive valuation multiples and strong returns offset by recent revenue and earnings declines and a high debt load.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported quarter (ending 2026-03-31) EPS of $1.07 beat the estimate of $1.02 by 4.8%. Next earnings date is 2026-07-28.
positives: Forward P/E of 12.10x suggests attractive valuation relative to expected future earnings.
Return on equity at 33.4% indicates strong profitability relative to shareholder equity.
High dividend yield of 6.9% offers significant income to shareholders.
Analyst consensus is a buy with a mean target price suggesting upside potential.
Last quarter's EPS beat analyst estimates, indicating positive short-term earnings performance.
negatives: Revenue growth of -1.6% and earnings growth of -27.2% year-over-year indicate a declining business.
Debt/Equity ratio of 181.51% signifies a highly leveraged balance sheet.
Operating margin of 6.3% and profit margin of 5.9% are relatively low.
Dividend payout ratio of 106.2% is unsustainable, suggesting potential future dividend cuts.
Analyst growth estimates for the current fiscal year are negative (-0.4%).

news.paragraph: Recent headlines for UPS focus heavily on an ongoing investigation into a fatal MD-11 crash, with reports highlighting maintenance lapses and similar past flaws that went unaddressed, raising questions for aviation officials. Separately, a new product announcement for a military-grade power supply also uses the UPS acronym, and one article suggests UPS could perform well in a post-Amazon landscape.

headlines.title: Maintenance Lapses Flagged in Lead Up to Fatal MD-11 Crash

PULL-UPS® DEBUTS LEARNING LAYER™ TECHNOLOGY, A NEW INNOVATION TO HELP KIDS LEARN THE DIFFERENCE BETWEEN WET AND DRY
UPS Could Thrive in a Post-Amazon World
